options: `@Valid` before `@RequestBody` — constraints only run when the parameter asks for validation
Nothing — constraint annotations on a DTO always run automatically
A manual `if (req.getName().isEmpty())` check — annotations are only documentation
`@NotBlank` belongs on the controller method, not on the DTO field
mcq.explanation_md: Constraint annotations describe the rules, but Spring only *enforces* them when the controller parameter is marked `@Valid` (or `@Validated`). Without it, the DTO deserializes and sails through — the exact bug in this question, and one of the most common real-world validation mistakes. The 'always automatic' option is tempting because the annotations *look* self-activating, but they're passive metadata until something triggers the validator. Manual `if` checks work but throw away the whole declarative system, and moving `@NotBlank` to the controller method isn't how field constraints are placed — rules live on the fields they guard.
